_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-11 Impedance Threshold Device (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-11: Impedance Threshold Device — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. The Impedance Threshold Device (ITD) enhances venous return during CPR decompression by restricting passive air entry into the lungs. It is used only in medical cardiac arrest for patients > 10 kg. Must not delay compressions if unavailable. ETCO2 must always be placed BETWEEN the ITD and the ventilation source.

sections.title: Indications and Contraindications
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-11: ITD — Indications and Contraindications
points: INDICATION: Medical cardiac arrest in patients weighing > 10 kg (22 lbs)

sections.title: Application on Face Mask (Without Advanced Airway)
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-11: ITD — Face Mask Application
points: Connect the ITD between the face mask and the BVM
Assembly order: Face Mask — ITD — BVM
Maintain a TIGHT mask seal throughout — the seal must be maintained for the device to function properly
Timing lights are NOT used when the ITD is on a face mask
Deliver ventilations at standard rates per Airway Management Protocol

sections.title: Application on ETT or Supraglottic Airway (Advanced Airway)
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-11: ITD — Advanced Airway Application
points: First confirm advanced airway placement with ETCO2 BEFORE attaching ITD
Assembly order (from patient to BVM): ETT or SGA — ITD — ETCO2 monitor — BVM
CRITICAL: ETCO2 monitor MUST be placed BETWEEN the ITD and the BVM (ventilation source) in all configurations
Enable the TIMING LIGHTS on the ITD device when used with an advanced airway (ETT or SGA)
Ventilate asynchronously — deliver ventilation of 1 second each time the timing light flashes
Do NOT over-ventilate — deliver only when the light flashes

sections.title: General Principles
points: Do NOT delay chest compressions if the ITD is unavailable — HQCPR takes priority
DISCONTINUE the ITD IMMEDIATELY upon ROSC (return of spontaneous circulation)
After ROSC: remove ITD from the airway circuit and continue ventilations per ROSC protocol
ETCO2 must always be between the ITD and the ventilation source regardless of airway type

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether the ITD is authorized for use in medical cardiac arrest patients > 10 kg
options: Authorized — ITD may be used per protocol
Not authorized — do not use ITD

deterioration_flags: ITD unavailable — do not delay compressions; proceed with standard cardiac arrest protocol without ITD
ROSC achieved — DISCONTINUE ITD IMMEDIATELY; remove from airway circuit; continue ventilations per ROSC protocol
Mask seal loss with face mask ITD — reposition mask; seal is essential for device function
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has selected ITD in protocol
Indication: medical cardiac arrest, patients > 10 kg (22 lbs)
Face mask: Mask — ITD — BVM; no timing lights
Advanced airway: ETT/SGA — ITD — ETCO2 — BVM; timing lights ON; ventilate 1 sec when light flashes
ETCO2 ALWAYS between ITD and BVM in all configurations
